```python
# test_title_case.py
import pytest

from your_module import title_case


@pytest.mark.parametrize(
    "inp, expected",
    [
        ("hello world", "Hello World"),                 # basic happy path
        ("HELLO WORLD", "Hello World"),                 # existing caps normalized
        ("a b c", "A B C"),                             # single-letter words
        ("  hello   world  ", "Hello World"),          # extra spaces collapse via split()
        ("python programming", "Python Programming"), # multiple words
        ("", ""),                                        # empty string
    ],
)
def test_title_case_happy_path(inp, expected):
    assert title_case(inp) == expected


@pytest.mark.parametrize(
    "inp, expected",
    [
        ("hello\tworld", "Hello World"),               # tabs are treated as whitespace by split()
        ("hello\nworld", "Hello World"),               # newlines as whitespace
        ("   ", ""),                                     # only whitespace -> no words
        (" leading and trailing ", "Leading And Trailing"),  # leading/trailing whitespace
        ("multi   space   test", "Multi Space Test"), # multiple internal spaces
    ],
)
def test_title_case_edge_cases_whitespace(inp, expected):
    assert title_case(inp) == expected


def test_title_case_error_non_string():
    with pytest.raises(AttributeError):
        # Non-strings will likely fail because .split() is not present
        title_case(None)


@pytest.mark.parametrize("bad", [123, 45.6, ["a", "b"], {"k": "v"}, object()])
def test_title_case_error_types(bad):
    with pytest.raises(AttributeError):
        title_case(bad)
```
